description.para: These functions will not return until transfer is complete

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *tx_bufs, const struct spi_buf_set *rx_bufs)
memberdef.name: spi_transceive

briefdescription.para: Read/write the specified amount of data from the SPI driver.

simplesect.para: This function is synchronous.

In master mode, the chip select line will remain asserted (active) for the entire duration of the transfer of all buffers in the provided buf sets. Only after all buffers have been transferred will CS be deasserted.

In peripheral mode, data transfer happens when the master asserts CS and provides the clock. The function will wait for the master to complete the transfer before returning. The CS is controlled by master and therefore may not be continuously asserted for the whole transfer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from, or NULL if none.
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to, or NULL if none.
Positive number of frames received in slave mode.
If successful in master mode.
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct spi_dt_spec *spec, const struct spi_buf_set *tx_bufs, const struct spi_buf_set *rx_bufs)
memberdef.name: spi_transceive_dt

briefdescription.para: Read/write data from an SPI bus specified in spi_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: spi_transceive(spec->bus, &spec->config, tx_bufs, rx_bufs);
spec
SPI specification from devicetree
tx_bufs
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from, or NULL if none.
rx_bufs
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to, or NULL if none.
a value from spi_transceive().

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *rx_bufs)
memberdef.name: spi_read

briefdescription.para: Read the specified amount of data from the SPI driver.

simplesect.para: This function is synchronous.
This function is a helper function calling spi_transceive.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to.
Positive number of frames received in slave mode.
If successful.
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Read data from a SPI bus specified in spi_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: spi_read(spec->bus, &spec->config, rx_bufs);
spec
SPI specification from devicetree
rx_bufs
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to.
a value from spi_read().

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *tx_bufs)
memberdef.name: spi_write

briefdescription.para: Write the specified amount of data from the SPI driver.

simplesect.para: This function is synchronous.
This function is a helper function calling spi_transceive.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from.
If successful.
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Write data to a SPI bus specified in spi_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: spi_write(spec->bus, &spec->config, tx_bufs);
spec
SPI specification from devicetree
tx_bufs
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from.
a value from spi_write().

description.para: With this API the transfer function will return after the transfer is started and report completion through a notification mechanism: callback or signal.

simplesect.para: Note that asynchronous API calls can still be blocking if the bus is already busy. The functions will block until the bus is available to start the requested transfer.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *tx_bufs, const struct spi_buf_set *rx_bufs, spi_callback_t callback, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: spi_transceive_cb

briefdescription.para: Read/write the specified amount of data from the SPI driver asynchronously.

simplesect.para: This function is available only if CONFIG_SPI_ASYNC is selected.
The chip select behavior as described by spi_transceive and the function of controller/peripheral modes is the same.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from, or NULL if none.
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to, or NULL if none.
Function pointer to completion callback. (Note: if NULL this function will not notify the end of the transaction, and whether it went successfully or not).
Userdata passed to callback
Positive number of frames received in slave mode.
If successful in master mode.
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *tx_bufs, const struct spi_buf_set *rx_bufs, struct k_poll_signal *sig)
memberdef.name: spi_transceive_signal

briefdescription.para: Read/write the specified amount of data from the SPI driver.

simplesect.para: This function is asynchronous.
The chip select behavior as described by spi_transceive and the function of controller/peripheral modes is the same.
This function is available only if CONFIG_SPI_ASYNC and CONFIG_POLL are selected.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from, or NULL if none.
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to, or NULL if none.
A pointer to a valid and ready to be signaled struct k_poll_signal. (Note: if NULL this function will not notify the end of the transaction, and whether it went successfully or not).
Positive number of frames received in slave mode.
If successful in master mode.
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *rx_bufs, struct k_poll_signal *sig)
memberdef.name: spi_read_signal

briefdescription.para: Read the specified amount of data from the SPI driver.

simplesect.para: This function is asynchronous.
This function is a helper function calling spi_transceive_signal.
This function is available only if CONFIG_SPI_ASYNC and CONFIG_POLL are selected.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be read will be written to.
A pointer to a valid and ready to be signaled struct k_poll_signal. (Note: if NULL this function will not notify the end of the transaction, and whether it went successfully or not).
Positive number of frames received in slave mode.
If successful
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct spi_config *config, const struct spi_buf_set *tx_bufs, struct k_poll_signal *sig)
memberdef.name: spi_write_signal

briefdescription.para: Write the specified amount of data from the SPI driver.

simplesect.para: This function is asynchronous.
This function is a helper function calling spi_transceive_signal.
This function is available only if CONFIG_SPI_ASYNC and CONFIG_POLL are selected.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance. Pointer-comparison may be used to detect changes from previous operations.
Buffer array where data to be sent originates from.
A pointer to a valid and ready to be signaled struct k_poll_signal. (Note: if NULL this function will not notify the end of the transaction, and whether it went successfully or not).
If successful.
means some part of the spi config is not supported either by the device hardware or the driver software.
means that some parameter of the spi_config is invalid.
Negative errno code on failure.

description.para: Theses functions are for using the SPI driver class through an RTIO-based API

briefdescription.para: Submit a SPI device with a request.

parameterdescription.para: Prepared submissions queue entry connected to an iodev defined by SPI_IODEV_DEFINE. Must live as long as the request is in flight.

briefdescription.para: Validate that SPI bus (and CS gpio if defined) is ready.

parameterdescription.para: SPI iodev defined with SPI_DT_IODEV_DEFINE
if the SPI bus is ready for use.
if the SPI bus (or the CS gpio defined) is not ready for use.

memberdef.name: SPI_DT_IODEV_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: const struct spi_dt_spec _spi_dt_spec_##name = \ SPI_DT_SPEC_GET(node_id, operation_, __VA_ARGS__); \ RTIO_IODEV_DEFINE(name, &spi_iodev_api, (void *)&_spi_dt_spec_##name)

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a given dt node on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: These do not need to be shared globally but doing so will save a small amount of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name to use for defining the iodev
Devicetree node identifier
SPI operational mode

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a devicetree instance on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to SPI_DT_IODEV_DEFINE(name, DT_DRV_INST(inst), operation).

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name to use for defining the iodev
Devicetree instance number
SPI operational mode

briefdescription.para: Master (controller) mode.

detaileddescription.para: In this case the device used with the API will function as a controller, meaning it will control the CLK line on the SPI bus and the chip select, and therefore have full control over the timing of the transaction.

briefdescription.para: Slave (peripheral) mode.

detaileddescription.para: With this mode, the device will function as a peripheral, meaning it will need to wait for it's select line to be asserted, and will be need to be subject to pacing by a controller's clock in order to send and receive data during a transaction. Slave mode.

briefdescription.para: Get SPI Operational mode bitmask from a spi_operation_t.
Clock Polarity (Clock Idle State)

detaileddescription.para: Used in spi_operation_t definition. If set, clock idle state will be 1 and active state will be 0. If unset, clock idle state will be 0 and active state will be 1. Unset is the default.

briefdescription.para: Clock Phase (Clock data capture edge)

detaileddescription.para: Used in spi_operation_t definition. If set, data is captured on transition from active to idle CLK state. If unset, data is captured on transition from idle to active state. Unset is the default.

briefdescription.para: Controller loopback mode.

detaileddescription.para: For testing purposes, enable hardware loopback, which means that transmit data is fed back to the receiver of the same controller.
Not all controllers support this feature.

briefdescription.para: Get SPI clock polarity and phase mode bitmask from a spi_operation_t.

description.para: A SPI Data word is a value that is shifted in/out of the controller's hardware FIFO and is the atomic unit of communication on the spi bus.
A word is also called a "data frame" in this API. A transfer is made up of an arbitrary number of words. The following options specify configurations of the SPI word for the operation.

briefdescription.para: Words are most significant bit first, used for spi_operation_t.
Words are least significant bit first, used for spi_operation_t.
Get SPI word size in bits from a spi_operation_t.

parameterdescription.para: A spi_operation_t from which to get the configured word size.

simplesect.para: The size (in bits) of a spi word for the operation.

briefdescription.para: Get a bitmask to set the word size in a spi_operation_t.

parameterdescription.para: The size of a SPI data frame in bits.

simplesect.para: A bitmask to apply to a spi_operation_t

briefdescription.para: Keep chip select active after transaction.

detaileddescription.para: After one of the spi transceive calls described in this API, if this flag is set in the spi config operation, then attempt to keep the CS active after the call, if supported and possible.

briefdescription.para: Retain ownership of the spi device.

detaileddescription.para: This is a software control parameter that will prevent the spi device from being accessed by other API callers after the transaction, and therefore should be used with caution.

The identifying piece of information for who "locks" the device is the spi_config pointer given to the transaction API, so this same config should be re-used to do another transaction or release the lock.
See spi_release for how to release the lock.

briefdescription.para: Chip select active state configuration.

detaileddescription.para: If this flag is set, the CS will be active high. If this flag is unset, the CS will be active low.
Default is active low (unset) as that is most common for spi peripherals.
Not all controllers are able to handle this natively, in which case a gpio can still be used to control the CS through software with a spi_cs_control

description.para: Some controllers support dual, quad or octal MISO lines connected to slaves.
Default is single, which is the case most of the time. Without CONFIG_SPI_EXTENDED_MODES being enabled, single is the only supported one.

briefdescription.para: Single line.
Dual lines.
Quad lines.
Octal lines.
Mask for MISO lines in spi_operation_t.
Get a struct gpio_dt_spec for a SPI device's chip select pin.

detaileddescription.para: Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

parameterdescription.para: a SPI device node identifier

simplesect.para: gpio_dt_spec struct corresponding with spi_dev's chip select

briefdescription.para: Get a struct gpio_dt_spec for a SPI device's chip select pin.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to SPI_CS_GPIOS_DT_SPEC_GET(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree instance number

simplesect.para: gpio_dt_spec struct corresponding with spi_dev's chip select

briefdescription.para: Initialize and get a pointer to a spi_cs_control from a devicetree node identifier.

detaileddescription.para: This helper is useful for initializing a device on a SPI bus. It initializes a struct spi_cs_control and returns a pointer to it. Here, node_id is a node identifier for a SPI device, not a SPI controller.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:
This example is roughly equivalent to:
For non-gpio CS, the idea is similar but the lead and lag fields of the cs struct will be populated instead.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for a device on a SPI bus

simplesect.para: a pointer to the spi_cs_control structure

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to a spi_cs_control from a devicetree node.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to SPI_CS_CONTROL_INIT(DT_DRV_INST(inst), delay).
Therefore, DT_DRV_COMPAT must already be defined before using this macro.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node instance number

simplesect.para: a pointer to the spi_cs_control structure

memberdef.name: SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_DEVICE_STATE_DEFINE(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)); \ Z_DEVICE_DEFINE(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), init_fn, deinit_fn, \ Z_DEVICE_DT_FLAGS(node_id), pm, data, config, \ level, prio, api, \ &Z_DEVICE_STATE_NAME(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)), \ __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Like DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() with SPI specifics.

detaileddescription.para: Defines a device which implements the SPI API. May generate a custom device_state container struct and init_fn wrapper when needed depending on SPI CONFIG_SPI_STATS.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.
Name of the init function of the driver.
Name of the deinit function of the driver.
PM device resources reference (NULL if device does not use PM).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Priority within the selected initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.

description.para: Some controllers support half duplex transfer, which results in 3-wire usage.
By default, full duplex will prevail.
2 frame formats are exposed: Motorola and TI.

The main difference is the behavior of the CS line. In Motorola it stays active the whole transfer. In TI, it's active only one serial clock period prior to actually make the transfer, it is thus inactive during the transfer, which ends when the clocks ends as well. By default, as it is the most commonly used, the Motorola frame format will prevail.

briefdescription.para: Opaque type to hold the SPI operation flags.
Check if SPI CS is controlled using a GPIO.

parameterdescription.para: SPI configuration.

simplesect.para: true If CS is controlled using a GPIO.
false If CS is controlled by hardware or any other means.

briefdescription.para: Check if SPI CS in spi_dt_spec is controlled using a GPIO.

parameterdescription.para: SPI specification from devicetree.

simplesect.para: true If CS is controlled using a GPIO.
false If CS is controlled by hardware or any other means.

briefdescription.para: Validate that SPI bus (and CS gpio if defined) is ready.

parameterdescription.para: SPI specification from devicetree
if the SPI bus is ready for use.
if the SPI bus (or the CS gpio defined) is not ready for use.

briefdescription.para: Release the SPI device locked on and/or the CS by the current config.

detaileddescription.para: Note: This synchronous function is used to release either the lock on the SPI device and/or the CS line that was kept if, and if only, given config parameter was the last one to be used (in any of the above functions) and if it has the SPI_LOCK_ON bit set and/or the SPI_HOLD_ON_CS bit set into its operation bits field. This can be used if the caller needs to keep its hand on the SPI device for consecutive transactions and/or if it needs the device to stay selected. Usually both bits will be used along each other, so the the device is locked and stays on until another operation is necessary or until it gets released with the present function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to a valid spi_config structure instance.
If successful.
Negative errno code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Release the SPI device specified in spi_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: spi_release(spec->bus, &spec->config);
spec
SPI specification from devicetree
a value from spi_release().

memberdef.name: SPI_CONFIG_DT
memberdef.initializer: { \ .frequency = DT_PROP(node_id, spi_max_frequency), \ .operation = (operation_) | \ DT_PROP(node_id, duplex) | \ DT_PROP(node_id, frame_format) | \ COND_CODE_1(DT_PROP(node_id, spi_cpol), SPI_MODE_CPOL, (0)) | \ COND_CODE_1(DT_PROP(node_id, spi_cpha), SPI_MODE_CPHA, (0)) | \ COND_CODE_1(DT_PROP(node_id, spi_hold_cs), SPI_HOLD_ON_CS, (0)) | \ COND_CODE_1(DT_PROP(node_id, spi_lsb_first), SPI_TRANSFER_LSB, (0)) | \ COND_CODE_1(DT_PROP(node_id, spi_cs_high), SPI_CS_ACTIVE_HIGH, (0)), \ .slave = DT_REG_ADDR(node_id), \ .cs = SPI_CS_CONTROL_INIT(node_id, __VA_ARGS__), \ .word_delay = DT_PROP(node_id, spi_interframe_delay_ns),\ }

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for spi_config from devicetree.

detaileddescription.para: This helper macro expands to a static initializer for a struct spi_config by reading the relevant frequency, slave, and cs data from the devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for the SPI device whose struct spi_config to create an initializer for
the desired operation field in the struct spi_config

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for spi_config from devicetree instance.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to SPI_CONFIG_DT(DT_DRV_INST(inst), operation_).

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree instance number
the desired operation field in the struct spi_config

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for spi_dt_spec from devicetree.

detaileddescription.para: This helper macro expands to a static initializer for a struct spi_dt_spec by reading the relevant bus, frequency, slave, and cs data from the devicetree.
Important: multiple fields are automatically constructed by this macro which must be checked before use. spi_is_ready_dt performs the required device_is_ready checks.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for the SPI device whose struct spi_dt_spec to create an initializer for
the desired operation field in the struct spi_config

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for spi_dt_spec from devicetree instance.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to SPI_DT_SPEC_GET(DT_DRV_INST(inst), operation_).

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree instance number
the desired operation field in the struct spi_config

briefdescription.para: Value that will never compare true with any valid overrun character.
The value sent on MOSI when all TX bytes are sent, but RX continues.

detaileddescription.para: For drivers where the MOSI line state when receiving is important, this value can be queried at compile-time to determine whether allocating a constant array is necessary.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for the SPI device to query
if controller does not export the value
default MOSI value otherwise

briefdescription.para: The value sent on MOSI when all TX bytes are sent, but RX continues.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to SPI_MOSI_OVERRUN_DT(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree instance number
if controller does not export the value
default MOSI value otherwise

memberdef.name: SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE(node_id, init_fn, NULL, pm, data, config, \ level, prio, api, __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Like DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() without deinit function.

detaileddescription.para: Defines a device which implements the SPI API. May generate a custom device_state container struct and init_fn wrapper when needed depending on SPI CONFIG_SPI_STATS.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.
Name of the init function of the driver.
PM device resources reference (NULL if device does not use PM).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Priority within the selected initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.

briefdescription.para: Like SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE(), but uses an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible instead of a node identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Instance number. The node_id argument to SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() is set to DT_DRV_INST(inst).
Other parameters as expected by SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Like SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE(), but uses an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible instead of a node identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Instance number. The node_id argument to SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() is set to DT_DRV_INST(inst).
Other parameters as expected by SPI_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI) controllers.
